steps.title: Locate the timing dispute
steps.content: Alvarez et al. connect the Mo increase around 2.5 billion years ago to an oxygen increase at that same time. Mehta et al. argue that later fluid movement through microfractures could have caused oxidative weathering long after deposition.

steps.title: Reject shared or irrelevant issues
steps.content: A asks about oxygen before 2.5 Ga, C concerns oxidative weathering itself, and D questions whether the Mo increase existed; those are not the central disagreement. The disputed issue is whether the Mo increase dates atmospheric oxygen to the same time. The answer is B.
